reviews.title: Remortgage
reviews.body: Jane looked after our mortgage when we bought our house and has done so again on our re-mortgage.  She is incredibly knowledgable and responsive, always at end of the phone when we needed her and always had the answers to our questions.  The process was very straight forward with her help.  Thank you again Jane!

reviews.title: SELF BUILD - NORMALLY DIFFICULT - NOT HERE
reviews.body: I made a phone call to apply for a self build mortgage  after speaking to a couple of other lenders first - within 5 minutes of speaking to a lady called Lana who has looked after me every single step of the way from start to finish ( and still looking after me now with the stage drawdowns etc ) I knew I was speaking to someone who I would be happy, relaxed and confident to do all of this with. Her help, perseverance and attention to detail is second to none - I will speak to Lana with every mortgage enquiry I have from now on......I couldn't possibly recommend her highly enough to do her justice.

reviews.title: Great Customer Service
reviews.body: The application process was straight forward and timely, I was given an initial appointment within two days for example and each of my messages during the process was answered the same day. Lana, who was my point of contact throughout was absolutely great, knowledgeable friendly and professional. I initially chose a fixed term discount product but as my sale was subsequently delayed, I was able to switch to a newly issued variable tracker at a couple of days notice. I would highly recommend Hanley for excellent customer service.

reviews.title: Can’t open account
reviews.body: I applied for a savings account, but first they sent me an email that I couldn’t open, I’m 80 years of age and it said something about needing an outlook account.My son helped me and said they want a passport and a driving licence and a photograph with me holding them up before the end of November. I haven’t got a passport and I’ve never drove so don’t have a driving license.I don’t have this problem with any other accounts I have. My son tried to help me but there’s nothing he could do either. I don’t understand why the Hanley are asking me to do this. I normally don’t have any problems with a credit check never owed anybody anything in my life. I shouldn’t fail and have never failed an identity check. I don’t understand what’s happened but looking at other reviews in a way I’m glad to see I’m not the only one. My son says they seem very amateurish and not a good building society to deal with so I’ll carry on saving with my local building society the Leeds. It’s just that I have to walk to crossgates and I’m not as good as waking as I use to be. My son is going to help me get online with Leeds building society. I just thought I’d give the Hanley a try as my daughter lives in Stoke and saves with them, I just like to put a few pounds away every month for christmas next year. Never mind there loss, not mine.i feel quite upset but will move on.

reviews.title: Would definately recommend
reviews.body: We dealt with Victoria Earl for our mortgage, from start to finish she was informative and helpful. She guided us through everything we needed to do. We have already recommended her to a friend. Thankyou for all your help.

reviews.title: It’s selfie time, smile for the camera, I think NOT!
reviews.body: I tried to open an on-line regular saver account. I’ve got several accounts with similar building societies and never had any problems opening them. I have lived at the same address for 30 years and I’m on the electoral roll. I have a few bank current accounts all on my credit reference file. I’m not in debt. I’ve never been refused credit or a bank account so applying for a savings account should be extremely straightforward but alas not with the Hanley. I’m even a previous account holder with the Hanley. It too was a monthly saver and when that matured I withdrew my savings without any problems, so what could go wrong?I applied online and paid an initial £300 deposit to open the account with my debit card. I then patiently waited for my welcome pack to arrive. To my surprise I received the following email.*****Just before we send out your welcome pack in the post, and to enable us to complete our identity checks, we will require some scanned identification from yourself. This will enable us to successfully complete your application.Please can you reply to this email with a photograph of your valid passport and driving licence as proof of person & address, and a photo of yourself holding the ID 'a selfie' for further verification.If you do not have either of these documents please do not hesitate to contact us to discuss alternative documents we can accept.If you require any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact the online banking team*****I was totally shocked, so a previous account holder they want a ‘selfie’ holding ID documents a passport in one hand and a driving licence in the other ? I just had to remind myself again, this wasn’t a bank account application or a mortgage application not even a credit card application. This was a monthly savings account application. This request is not just unprofessional and unreasonable in my opinion it’s a downright disgraceful and insulting request. Email is also NOT a secure means of communication. I of course reached out for assistance by phone, but they could offer no help. In order to proceed I had to provide the above which I said, simply do a soft credit check on me and you can verify my details, you also have them on my previous account number #####. I don’t have a problem supplying ID, in fact note I have done so already for Trustpilot, but this was completed in a secure fashion. With Hanley refusing to budge I reluctantly offered to supply details to a secure third party like ‘Onfido’ but that was also refused, I don’t know how much more reasonable I could have been.I felt that I had no choice but to ask for my initial deposit of £300 back which no doubt I’ll get but not received as I write this review. EDIT :- now received backIt’s such a shame as I like to support the smaller building societies like Hanley.To attract savers, they need to be professional and use secure means of communication with respect of documents or simply use the services of another professional ID screening service, taking selfies holding documents and sending by email is neither secure or a professional way to check an applicants ID.

reviews.title: What I would like to say about Hanley…
reviews.body: What I would like to say about Hanley Economic Building Society.I was looking for a product that suited my needs,I viewed a lot of other providers and they fell short of what I was looking for,I found that Hanley Economic Building Society had the very product i was looking for,They were very  easy to talk to and the process was straight forward, simple and easy.John Brand

reviews.title: Great customer service
reviews.body: We found Hanley to be efficient in handling the mortgage and their staff friendly and helpful. A particular thank you to Lana Beech, who responded quickly to any queries and problems and was always on top of the process.

reviews.title: 10/10 service from victoria
reviews.body: I first made contact with victoria a few months ago, and she's been very helpful and supportive. She was always at end of the phone or email to help or explain anything to me. She literally made my dream a reality and im very grateful.

reviews.title: Traditional values
reviews.body: Traditional values,competitive products helpful friendly staff.

reviews.title: Mortgage Advice
reviews.body: Excellent help and advice from Vicki Earl in our pursuit for a mortgage. From start to finish, experience was brilliant.

reviews.title: Self-Build Mortgage Application
reviews.body: To date, Lana and HEBS have been very diligent, provided excellent guidance and explanations, and communicated clearly and responsively throughout the process of my self-build application. I was originally apprehensive of utilising a self-build mortgage for my project, having been told by other funding providers of their complexity and potential "red-tape". However, my experience with HEBS couldn't have been further from this. I wouldn't hesitate to use them again, and would highly recommended them to anyone else embarking on a self-build project!

reviews.title: 2025 RIO Mortgage Application
reviews.body: We have just completed on a Hanley RIO Mortgage. The Mortgage Advisor, Lana Beech, was particularly helpful, polite & efficient answering most queries within an hour of them being posed. This made our application straightforward & trouble free. Lana is an asset to The Hanley Economic Building Society with her pleasant manner & interest in the customer. Well done & thank you.

reviews.title: Met with mortgage advisor Jane who was…
reviews.body: Met with mortgage advisor Jane who was extremely kind polite and helpful

reviews.title: I called to enquire about self build…
reviews.body: I called to enquire about self build mortgages. Having never had one before we were completely in the dark. Lana Beech was our contact at the Hanley. She was absolutely brilliant. Lana helped us navigate through and was always on hand to help answer our questions….. of which there were a lot! And coordinate instructions with our solicitor.Lana made what could have been a stressful time so much easier.We would highly recommend the Hanley and if you’re lucky, you’ll get Lana.

reviews.title: Great Service!
reviews.body: Great service provided by The Hanley! The process was very smooth and dealt with efficiently. Jane was great with us from inception to completion. Thanks again!

reviews.title: Excellent service.
reviews.body: From the beginning of the process right to the end, Jane kept us well informed and was passionate about helping us with our first mortgage. Jane was always available when needed and got back to us straight away with updates and support. We could not recommend Hanley building society enough.

reviews.title: Lana was excellent from the day we…
reviews.body: Lana was excellent from the day we first made contact until completion. She was extremely knowledgeable and gave us all the information in an easy to understand way, she was very quick to reply to any of our queries and really cared about us and our plans. Thank you again Lana, you’re a credit to Hanley

reviews.title: Jane Longmore was amazing!
reviews.body: Jane Longmore was amazing. As an older couple looking to buy our first home together, Jane held our hand throughout the whole process. The Hanley Economic offered us a longer mortgage with a 5% deposit. Other banks were not so generous. We have been able to move out of rented into our first home together. It is amazing to be paying off our own mortgage rather than our old landlords. Jane's customer service and attention to detail was second to none, she even sent us a card when we moved in. The service did not stop with Jane, all the staff involved in our application were fabulous. I would recommend Jane and the Hanley Economic to anyone. Its also great to support a local business with local people. It felt like the good old days where you had human interaction and not a computer says no attitude.Thank you Jane and the Hanley Economic 🥰

reviews.title: Great communication
reviews.body: Great communication, Jane engaged with us, providing clear explanations at each stage in the process, answering all our questions confirming that we have made the right decision to remortgage with the Hanley Economic. Overall the remortgage process went smoothly, enabling us to complete on the required date.

reviews.title: Friendly and helpful staff
reviews.body: Friendly and helpful staff. A pleasure to find an alternative to the banks we no longer have in town.

reviews.title: Personal service with excellent communication.
reviews.body: Jane has been absolutely amazing, start to finish. I never thought home ownership would be an option as a single applicant but I am now the proud owner of my own home. The advice given by Jane has been invaluable, making my new home affordable and the right choice for me. Her communication has been fantastic throughout and has made the process easy. I am forever grateful and can’t thank Jane enough. The right choice if you’re looking for a local bank who genuinely care about their customers.

reviews.title: HEBS have closed the Cheadle branch.
reviews.body: Unfortunately, and due to circumstances beyond their control, Hanley Economic Building Society had to close their office in my home town of Cheadle at the end of 2024 - and as of yet, they haven't taken steps to open another in the town - which is why I can't give them 5 stars.Having said that, whilst recently having my car serviced at a garage in Stone, and as I needed to make some amendments to my accounts, I decided to call in at the Stone branch.I am pleased to report that I received excellent service and the customer adviser was able to resolve the issues regarding my accounts to my complete satisfaction.However, I would really appreciate if at some point in the near future, HEBS could re-establish a site presence in Cheadle.

reviews.title: Remortgage
reviews.body: I decided to apply for a mortgage with The Hanley Economic when my fixed rate mortgage deal with another lender was coming to an end. I first heard of The Hanley by browsing on the internet and liked the clear information given on their web site.After calling them I was assigned to Jane a Mortgage advisor. A telephone appointment was arranged and Jane was just brilliant. Jane talked through the product I was interested in and spent time getting to know our circumstances and objectives to ensure the mortgage we were interested in met our requirements.Nothing was too much trouble for Jane. She was so patient and caring and made the experience as stress-free as possible. I immediately felt my decision to chose The Hanley was the right one. I felt valued as a prospective client and unlike so many Banks and Societies the attention to detail and time spent explaining things was first rate. Jane was available to answer the few queries we subsequently had.I would have no hesitation recommending The Hanley based on my experience. Thanks Jane The Hanley is fortunate to have you

reviews.title: Excellent friendly staff
reviews.body: Excellent friendly staff, process thoroughly explained.Knocked 1 star off as new account opening was rather long winded and you had to make an appointment.

reviews.title: Very helpful
reviews.body: When you go in the staff are always helpful and polite can't do enough for you I would recommend it to anyone

reviews.title: Why I love the Hanley
reviews.body: A very spacious, clean and welcoming environment very close to the Stone main street. Very pleasant, polite, welcoming staff of smart appearance and helpful and knowledgeable. A credit to themselves and the Hanley. I have been with the  Hanley for many years.  The Stone branch is a very important convenient facility to me and my wife and an asset to the town and community.

reviews.title: Excellent customer service
reviews.body: We find the staff in your Newcastle branch knowledgeable and friendly.

reviews.title: As always service above and beyond
reviews.body: As always service above and beyond - always a great service and staff

reviews.title: Staff very knowledgeable
reviews.body: Staff very knowledgeable,  helpful & smiled & made me feel very welcome. The impression given was that the customer was very important to them . So a very comfortable / pleasant experience with the outcome I was hoping for .

reviews.title: I was given an exact meeting time
reviews.body: I was given an exact meeting time. I was taken into a private office. All my requirements were listened to. Everything was professional.

reviews.title: Great product backed by superb service
reviews.body: Jane Longmore was our mortgage advisor at the Hanley for our recent purchase of a smallholding in Somerset. We chose the Hanley for their best in market lifetime tracker mortgage. Jane then delighted us with her speedy attention and responsiveness. We had some complications in the run up to completion and Jane and the rest of the team were nothing but helpful at a stressful time. Highly recommended.

reviews.title: OUTSTANDING Service and Care
reviews.body: OUTSTANDING Service and CareJane at the Hanley, was outstanding.  We found the 100 percent mortgage product on an advert as a good option to get on the property ladder after some personal challenges.Jane kept us updated on the situation with our application, was helpful, informative and friendly and there is nothing about the service i would improve.10/10Thanks Jane & The Hanley for getting me and my little family our own home finally.

reviews.title: We had a great experience with Lana…
reviews.body: We had a great experience with Lana when we recently completed our mortgage application. She has been helpful, patient and very informative throughout the process. Nothing was too much trouble. Thank you Lana for making a sometimes dauting task so seamless!

reviews.title: My experience was excellent with the…
reviews.body: My experience was excellent with the staff.

reviews.title: I had the most wonderful experience…
reviews.body: I had the most wonderful experience using Hanley Economic - from Lana who did a fantastic job in the early stages up until my mortgage was approved and the completions team who were so supportive when I was up against hurdles. I would thoroughly, thoroughly recommend Hanley who were attentive, seamless but also so incredibly friendly throughout.

reviews.title: The process was easy and everything we…
reviews.body: The process was easy and everything we asked for was dealt with efficiently

reviews.title: Staff
reviews.body: Staff always helpful and show a genuine interest in customers.

reviews.title: Honest banking they give me good advice…
reviews.body: Honest banking they give me good advice on my money.

reviews.title: Always personal approach
reviews.body: Always personal approach, super helpful whether your account balance is £500 or £100k ( my actual experience).Staff go all out to make the process clear and easy. Special thanks to Julie Barlow, who is just exceptional.  Thank you

reviews.title: Staff Went out of their way to be…
reviews.body: Staff Went out of their way to be accommodating for us

reviews.title: Everyone at the branches we use are…
reviews.body: Everyone at the branches we use are very helpful and polite

reviews.title: Excellent service
reviews.body: Meg was efficient and really friendly. She helped us with everything we needed with a gorgeous smile. Thank you so much!

reviews.title: Support of those who think a little differently
reviews.body: I support adults with learning disabilities to the Stone branch regularly. Recently I supported a gentleman who became quite distressed and his comments were inappropriate. We were being served by Meg and her response was absolutely amazing, she continued very professionally with her role and continued to engage with the gentleman in her professional capacity. No over reaction. Meg is a credit to the branch as are all the staff their professionalism shines through their empathic values and understanding

reviews.title: As a first time buyer I was…
reviews.body: As a first time buyer I was understandably nervous about the whole mortgage process, but Hanley Economic made everything so smooth and straightforward. Lana was incredibly helpful and reassuring every step of the way. She took the time to explain everything clearly, answered all my questions and made me feel at ease! I couldn’t have asked for a better experience. Thanks Lana!

reviews.title: Fantastic job on my remortgage
reviews.body: The process for my remortgage application has been great.Jane has gone out of her way to make time to process everything as quickly and as smoothly as possible. I cannot fault anything and would highly recommend for anyone looking for a mortgage to contact the Hanley Economic for a fantastic service

reviews.title: Great customer service - Lana
reviews.body: Over it was good but working closely with Lana she made everything start to finish really easy and helpful. She was nice, caring and clear and made sure she advised us on things that we needed to take into consideration. I cannot thank her enough for the amount of effort and time she took out of her day to make sure everything went as smooth as possible, she's a real asset to the company and I would happily recommend my friends and family use her in the future.

reviews.title: First-Class Service from Hanley and Lana
reviews.body: I recently completed a remortgage through the Hanley Economic Building Society and was expertly guided through the entire process by their mortgage advisor, Lana. As remortgages can often be lengthy and complex, I was really grateful for Lana’s professional and proactive support.From the outset, Lana clearly explained the process, presented me with a range of mortgage options tailored to my needs, and ensured I had all the information I needed to make an informed decision. Once I selected the mortgage I wanted to proceed with, Lana handled everything efficiently and kept me regularly updated at each stage.Lana was a pleasure to deal with, always available by phone or email to answer any queries promptly and clearly. I can’t recommend the Hanley Economic Building Society, and Lana in particular, highly enough.

reviews.title: Always nice to go into HEBS
reviews.body: I have been using Hanley Economic Building Society for a number of years now. I do prefer having a passbook and can also check on my online banking if I need to. The mortgage rates also seem very competitive especially for first time buyers and I encouraged my children to open accounts and they are now both on the property ladder with The Hanley's help. I find it nice to still be able to go into the branch and deal with very helpful and friendly staff. Well done Hanley Economic Building Society keep up the good work.

reviews.title: Lana was so helpful and informative…
reviews.body: Lana was so helpful and informative throughout the process. Answered all our questions clearly. Thank you Lana!

reviews.title: Amazing service very friendly staff…
reviews.body: Amazing service very friendly staff that made me feel at ease

reviews.title: Highly recommend!
reviews.body: As a first-time buyer, the mortgage process felt a bit daunting at first – but Lana made everything so much easier. She was incredibly helpful, patient, and always happy to explain things clearly. Her support gave me real confidence throughout the journey, and I can’t thank her enough for making the whole experience smooth and stress-free. Highly recommend!

reviews.title: Not recommended for mortgages
reviews.body: Not recommended for mortgages.  Although the products look good, the Hanley's unethical (IMO) treatment of customers might be questioned.  Although they protested, when questioned, that they don't always increase their SVR when the bank rate increases, in my experience this only happened once.  However, they didn't reduce their SVR in response to any of the three recent bank rate reductions.  What makes this shameful (IMO) is that they are now offering new customers significantly better discounts from SVR compared to the discount given to existing customers - and we weren't allowed to move our mortgage to any of the new products!  So we were stuck.  Needless to say, we have now moved to a different building society, saving £175 per MONTH in interest payments!Insurance companies aren't allowed to offer preferential rates to new customers, so how come building societies can get away with it?

reviews.title: Ripped us off by £840 for a mortgage application thatey knew they wouldn't accept, Robbing cowboys
reviews.body: Terrible, incapable robbers. They took £840 off us for an application for a mortgage and rejected the application immediately without communication, proper review of our application  evidence or consultation, They had no intention of lending us the money even though our deposit was 50% of what we wanted to lend. If they did this to just 100 customers they would make a neat £80k without lifting a finger, corrupt and greedy

reviews.title: This is a rather curious outfit, perhaps an understatement
reviews.body: This is a rather curious outfit. Perhaps an understatement.I opened the Branch Saver in 2023 - this took forever and was very stressful. Apparently they were short staffed and the account was massively subscribed. Presumably they hadn't envisaged this happening.However, the weirdest thing ever, and extraordinarily out of line, is that I have never once received any notification of any drop in interest rate on the account. This failure is of course totally out of line with required practice, so I'm at a loss to understand how this building society thinks this is acceptable.Having checked in February about the interest on the account, today, again when I went online I found it had dropped again.  Are they not aware that this is contrary to their obligation to their customers?

reviews.title: Great experience with mortgage…
reviews.body: Great experience with mortgage application. Staff were fab and dealt with me an an individual and supported me through the journey.

reviews.title: Mortgage Rate disparity
reviews.body: Following on from DG's review below i alos have a RIO.I have contacted the Hanley today to enquire as to when they will be adjusting their SVR to reflect the 3 reductions in base rates.I have been told today that they have no intention of reducing their rates to reflect the cuts but are keeping it under review.I havent checked their savings rates but i am wondering if it is possible that savings rates have have been reduced to reflect the cuts?Is it possible that The Hanley is bolstering their margin to the disadvantage of those on SVR rate mortgages?Time for pastures new I think!

reviews.title: Failure to pass on BoE rate reductions
reviews.body: I have a lifetime discounted mortgage with the Hanley that started approximately 2 years ago when the rate was very competitive. I think this attracted a lot of customers as they were very busy at the time I applied.They subsequently increased the rate with the BoE base rate rises, which was expected but still kept them competitive.This problem is that the base rate has now dropped 3 times since August 2024 and they have failed to adjust their SVR accordingly leaving customers like me on a higher uncompetitive rate. Most other lenders have lowered their SVR in line with the BoE base rate reductions.I'd really hoped that this was a mortgage I could stay on for the full term (20 years) rather than having to switch every few years but there are now much cheaper deals out there (0.75% less) so it makes sense to look elsewhere.

reviews.title: Shocking service
reviews.body: Shocking service. Longstanding customer of 7 years and I had erroneous activity on my bank and payments got cancelled luckily Halifax was on it. Made aware by  Harley their  DD was cancelled so contacted them got it sorted or so I thought.Now in the call no one said anything about a double payment just that they’d take the next payment out in the 1st of the month. Next thing threatening letter (never had a missed payment ever with them) so I paid it as soon as they clued me in to what they wanted. Emailed them to tell them how disappointed I was in them. Cue 2 dismissive and quite bullish letters from them.I worked 2 jobs one full time the other 2 days on a weekend to avoid not paying. To get a letter making out after 7 years not a missed payment questioning my money sense, finger pointing and just having a bad customer service approach. I was going to stay until this. Not now. Don’t trust.

reviews.title: Wasted our time lost 4 weeks waiting…
reviews.body: Wasted our time lost 4 weeks waiting for them in end went elsewhere and had a decision in 48 hours. Awful be warned.

reviews.title: Smooth application process but not passing on lower borrowing costs
reviews.body: Recently took out a new mortgage on a discounted variable rate which was advertised in July and completed in August. Went through a broker London and Country who were great and the communication asking for documents was smooth between lender and broker. Had to have a revised offer due to an error on L&C's part which was resolved with 2 working days. The only downfall is that since my application there has not been a reduction in their SVR following 2 BOE rate cuts. I appreciate that the rate is not directly linked to the BOE Rate, however they are one of only a small minority of lenders that have not reduced their rates which I find very disappointing. L&C have a 'SVR watch' page on their website which I have been checking so I know they are the minority.

reviews.title: Disappointingly behind the times.
reviews.body: I like the staff, the mortgage products were excellent (when I had one) and they were really accommodating about the amount I could borrow & any changes I needed to make over the term.It’s great that our small town still has a branch and I do appreciate it very much. However, they’re so far behind the times where the banking apps and savings books are concerned, and interest rates for savings accounts are fairly low.They still insist on people using passbooks and there’s a £10 charge if you lose them. I can’t say I’ve ever had a bank/building society which has done this. They also post books out, so we’d have to pay if the passbook was lost in the post, which I don’t feel is acceptable.You can’t make any withdrawals (even from instant accounts) without the passbook, not even if you have other forms of ID and if you ask them to transfer the money to an account it came from. I’ve been told I need to close the account to access it if I don’t want to pay the £10 Lost Book Fee. The online banking app doesn’t work for all accounts. This account allows for instant access, but you can’t transfer any money using the app.All in all, it’s such a shame they’re so far behind and such dinosaurs.

reviews.title: Poor online service
reviews.body: The online functionality is incredibly poor and light years behind its competitors. Often crashes when trying to log in. So slow that you can literally go and make a cup of tea whilst waiting in between screens. Whenever I transfer money it takes an age and then gives an error message saying it did not work. The first few times I tried again but ended up transferring the money twice. I quickly figured out the error message is in-fact, ironically, an error and the money was transferred. Overall, in a modern world, the online service is woefully lacking!

reviews.title: Disrespectful customer service
reviews.body: Myself and my cousin  visited the Hanley Economic to disclose a Bereavement. We thought we would be taken into A room to go through my late mum's accounts but it was all done in reception area. What happened to confidentiality and respect. The lady from the bereavement team didn't even say 'I'm sorry for your loss'. And yet other banks and companies have been sincerely sorry and respectful. The lady 'pulled her face' and when she said did I want a cheque or bank transfer costing £25 I said 'is it ok if I make a quick call to my bank' She pulled her face again.We were glad to get out of there. The vibes this lady was giving off weren't of a friendly nature by any means and to have to deal with my mum's financial affairs in reception is disrespectful and dreadful customer service. If I was attending Hanley Economic days after my mum's passing, I would have walked out. I will be submitting a complaint.

reviews.title: Awful lender - AVOID
reviews.body: Took a month to review my personal mortgage application and then rejected it because I have a BTL within my limited company (which runs at a profit).Avoid these utter cretins at all costs.

reviews.title: Lana is a credit to the company
reviews.body: We completed our mortgage application with Lana. To say she was amazing is an understatement. She was so lovely and friendly, she actually felt like a friend who was truly happy for us throughout our application process. She was professional and explained everything throughout and at every stage so we always knew what was happening. I would recommend the hanley as the products are competitive and Lana is an excellent mortgage advisor who is a credit to the society.Thankyou so much.

reviews.title: I opened a fixed 3 year fixed bond by…
reviews.body: I opened a fixed 3 year fixed bond by post .Very competitive rates ( best I found actually )When I’ve had cause to call to clarify any points or check deposits received ok , they answer very promptly and could not be more helpful or friendly

reviews.title: Why are we waiting
reviews.body: I opened a savings account online on 21st Dec and nothing happens  - finally discovered their chat line on 12th Jan, but a conversation takes  forever as if one person is chatting to several customers at the same time.Finally she gets my account opened, but tells me the introductory letter might take weeks to get to me, so she will give me details so I can pay money in straight away, ' but I waited forever and no details came and I had to go out.

reviews.title: Excellent customer service in the…
reviews.body: Excellent customer service in the Newcastle Branch today. Thank you

reviews.title: Avoid like the plague
reviews.body: Basically a racket!  Had a 2 year fixed with Hanley which wasn’t really fixed for two years at all only for 18 months. Have sorted a remortgage as their nee fixed rates were pathetic. Hanley sent my new lender the wrong redemption statement which means I couldn’t complete on the intended day. Not only that but Hanley are charging me £48 a day in interest until my mortgage switches over. It’s ridiculous! Hanley probably won’t reply to this review as they are too busy ripping people off!!

reviews.title: Excellent customer service
reviews.body: Excellent customer service. I have a postal branch saver account, but I needed to get the money out quickly. Yes I had to send in my passbook, but was able to it out using get a CHAPS payment, yes there was a fee but that’s my fault for not being more organised. Great to be able to deal with a real person, not a chat bot, who sorted the issue for me. Many thanks.

reviews.title: Opened a Branch Saver account back in…
reviews.body: Opened a Branch Saver account back in 2018 which tracks the Bank of England base rates for savers, Hanley were happy to take my money while interest rates were at a all time low but as soonas interest rates start to recover for savers they introduce new terms and conditions which now restricts the amount in your account and freezing any future deposits.Not a great way to keep loyal customers.

reviews.title: Hard to close account and get money back
reviews.body: Like some other reviewers on here it took several weeks to open my account which was very frustrating. During this process I was assured on the phone that this was due to exceptional circumstances and that closing the account would not be subject to the same delays.I now need the money I deposited in this account so that I can complete the purchase of a house. I called them about this and was told that in they charge a fee for transferring out when you close the account, but this can be avoided if you write to them and enclose your passbook, upon receipt they will then send you a cheque.It has now been over a week since I did this and I have heard nothing from them. Their online chat is not responding and when I call on the phone it just drops.I am hoping that they read their trust pilot reviews and will now contact me, otherwise I guess my only option is the FCA. I just want my money back.

reviews.title: They take far too long to open an…
reviews.body: They take far too long to open an account by post .I sent them a cheque on the 31 May . It is now 15 Juneand still my  account has not been opened . Meanwhile , I have lost any potential interest I may have earned elsewhere . I shall close it, when it is opened and put my money elsewhere

reviews.title: What idiot chief exec came up with this plan??
reviews.body: The Hanley launched a high interest product in May which obviously proved very popular - far too popular for this little company to deal with.16 days after I posted my cheque I have no word from them & the money is still sitting in my bank earning 0% interest. That's a lot of lost interest so not a great start.Website says  'Don't bother calling because we can't answer any queries'.What idiot chief exec came up with this plan ???

reviews.title: Excellent service at their Stone branch
reviews.body: Excellent service at their Stone branch. I had almost forgotten what good professional service is like. Really good to rediscover it again.

reviews.title: Excellent Customer Service
reviews.body: I initially contacted Hanley because I was attracted by their mortgage products which were very competitive at the time I applied.  I received excellent customer service from start to finish with the processing of my mortgage application.  The staff were always polite, friendly, very knowledgeable and nothing was too much trouble.  Special thanks to Stacey for pulling out all the stops to arrange my mortgage appointment at short notice, and to Lana for expertly guiding me through the process.

reviews.title: I have had several savings accounts…
reviews.body: I have had several savings accounts with the Hanley Economic Building Society for over 30 years. I regularly post my passbooks to their head office and up until recently I have not had any particular problems with this arrangement. In December I posted one of my passbooks but when this wasn’t returned I contacted HEBS customer services. I was told that they could not trace having received the passbook and that I should call into one of their Branches with my passport of photo ID to get a replacement. When I visited the Stone branch I was told that I could have a new passbook but I would have to pay £10 for the privilege. To be honest if it was my fault I would have probably paid the £10 but in the circumstances I did not think that a charge should be applied bearing in mind the length of time I have been a ‘member’ ( I even had my mortgage with them!) The cashier spoke to a supervisor who re-confirmed that the £10 charge would apply and no concessions could be made. Due to the way this was dealt with I decided to close all my accounts so after 30 years with the ‘Hanley Economic Building Society’ it was deemed that £10 was more important to them than to continue to have me as a ‘member’ Needless to say, from my personal experience, I could never recommend anyone becomes a ‘Hanley’ member or uses any of their products or services. Poor show!

reviews.title: Poor Interest Rates Nov 2022
reviews.body: I like the Hanley, I like having funds in totally offline passbooks, but the interest rates mean that we will be moving part of our savings elsewhere (Vernon BS in Stockport - yes, just in border). Why won't the Hanley offer 2% interest on a 35 day notification account? (passbook). It's ok putting things on Facebook about self build mortgages etc, but what about the people who supply this cash?

reviews.title: Time to Leave.
reviews.body: Dreadful saving interest rates. Shocking customer service. Outdated processes. Therefore time to leave after less 10 months.

reviews.title: Excellent and accessible service
reviews.body: I've found their mortgage service to be highly accessible.  When I call them I get to talk to a real person, without the annoying call centre experience.  Excellent customer service.  Have had no trouble with them,

reviews.title: Avoid!!!
reviews.body: The Hanley said they would remove my ex-partner from our self-build mortgage if I met their affordability criteria. I met their criteria and then they moved their self-defined goalposts as it, 'increased their risk'. As a consequence neither of us could move on with our lives.They then refused release of final stage payment unless I paid another exorbitant fee.The Hanley severely damaged my health and delayed the project significantly all while I was paying them ~6% interest.They had the following statement on their website, “Ensuring the customer is at the heart of everything we do”. It couldn't be further from the truth.They knew the potential consequences of their actions, they didn't care. Avoid.

reviews.title: Disappointed  my son and girlfriend…
reviews.body: Disappointed  my son and girlfriend looking to buy their 1 St house we recommended the hanley because we'd had 2 mortgages over27 yrs with them also they were doing 5% deposit mortgage. They saw a few houses then found the 5% had stopped so couldn't afford to look at anymore houses. Then 5% started up again they had the funds and looked at loads of properties which quite a few were outbid on and we're sold immediately. Disappointing times so when they found a property yesterday that the owner accepted their offer  they were overjoyed. They had a mortgage in principal already in place to the value they wanted and appointed a solicitor submitted the relevant paperwork  this morning only to be told 2 hrs later that the hanley had dropped the 5% deposit offer this morning and would not honor it another disappointing blow after weeks and weeks of searching and let downs now let down by a building society we trusted for many years no forewarning or nothing very disappointing and would not recommend
